rules: YOUR ONE STOP NOSEY SHOP! NOSEY NEIGHBORS GROUP, with an added buy and sell option, Baby! - a place to share info about what is happening in Bowling Green, OH! It can be reporting foodbanks, crimes, news, alerts, reviewing restaurants, traffic, emergencies etc....basically any information that'll help neighbors stay informed of what's going on in Bowling Green--and buy and sell while you are at it! The main rule here is while you can inform and share your opinion, 👉We would like to limit negativity and be as positive as you can, as we want to spread unity, not division! The mission of this group is to inform and unite Bowling Green and the surrounding! So I'm Hoping this group has ALOT...not all....but ALOT of positivity.....instead of negativity. Like bringing the positive NOSY stuff to light. Down to filming someone opening a door in public FOR SOMEONE ELSE... to watching someone else pay for another's McDonalds Meal......here locally.

name: Piqua, OH
rules:
This is a group for residents (past and present) and neighbors of Piqua, OH to discuss anything pertaining to Piqua such as photos, community events, ask questions, voice concerns, make suggestions, etc.
We stand for LIMITED censorship, but please do not confuse this with being an uncensored group. We do not want to have to delete any posts, though to keep things running smoothly we do have a list of simple rules that we will be enforcing a bit more heavily moving forward. Please review them before posting.
If you see any posts that violate our rules, please feel free to use the report feature and we will review it. Please do not repeatedly report the same post. If we review it and choose to keep it, annoying us with reports will not change our minds but may result in your removal.
Unlike another Piqua group who bans people for literally no reason, we have no desire to moderate with an iron fist or ban people simply for disagreeing with us. We welcome everyone who's willing to follow our rules. Anyone who violates our rules will receive a warning, usually in the form of a post deletion notice. Those who continue to violate our rules after being warned, may end up with their accounts muted or banned from the group. Removals and bans will, however, be utilized only if we feel no other recourse would suffice. Blocking admins/mods will result in an automatic ban once discovered.
Please keep in mind this group is NOT run by nor affiliated with the City of Piqua government in any way and any posts you see here by us or anyone who may be employed by the City of Piqua are our own personal opinions and not to be construed as official city policy or statements unless directly stated as such.

name: I live in Sidney, OH
rules:
Invite anyone you know who currently lives in Sidney.
Sidney is the county seat of Shelby County, Ohio. William Stewart was the first person to settle in the area of Sidney, but residents did not establish the town until 1819. Residents named the town after Sir Philip Sidney, a resident of Great Britain.
Sidney grew relatively quickly, having a population of 713 people in 1840. This growth was partly due to a canal that connected Sidney with the Miami and Erie Canal, providing local residents access to markets across the United States. In 1846, Sidney contained two newspaper offices, five churches, eighteen stores, one oil well, two carding mills, one fulling mill, three flourmills, and four sawmills.
Over the next several decades, Sidney continued to grow. In 1880, the town's population was 3,823 people, with over one-third of the residents being school-aged children. Sidney was now a center of agricultural trade, with the canal and two railroad lines servicing the community. In 1888, two newspaper offices, ten churches, two banks, and numerous manufacturing businesses existed in the community. Most businesses provided services or products to farmers in the surrounding countryside. Sidney's largest employer was John Loughlin, a school furniture manufacturer, who employed 147 workers.
During the twentieth and early twenty-first centuries, Sidney has remained an important economic center. Most Shelby County residents are engaged in agriculture. Farmers rely on Sidney businesses for agricultural tools and other products. Sidney, with a population of 20,211 people in 2000, was the only Shelby County town with more than two thousand workers. Almost forty percent of all Shelby County residents live in Sidney

name: Salem Ohio Community Chat
rules:
Welcome to the Salem Ohio Ties Group!
This group is for anyone with any connection to Salem, Ohio — whether you were born and raised there, have family or friends in town, moved away years ago, or just feel that Salem will always be “home” no matter where life takes you.
It doesn’t matter if you’re still living in Salem, scattered across the country, or even across the world. If Salem has a place in your heart or your story, you belong here.
Feel free to post memories, photos, news, events, or anything that reminds you of home. The places you share don’t have to be strictly in Salem — if it’s connected to our people, our stories, or our community, it’s welcome.
Salem will always be our hometown. Let’s keep the connection alive! ❤️
Drop a comment and tell us how you’re tied to Salem

I encourage everyone to interact and take part in the daily postings of the group. The intent of this group is to support local commerce

name: Allen County ( Lima, Ohio ) & Surrounding areas Freebies
rules:
FIRST there doesnt need to be "rules"....Understand that we are adults... But with that being said ill set some guidlines that i would hope you plan on following... this group is to post if you have something for free that you would like to give away to someone who can use it. One mans junk is another mans treasure. Its a first come first serve bases. Please dont be rude to one another. If things start to get out of hand... we will make rules... and we will start banning people...
ALSO!!! Please only take 1-2 free items per day. I do not want the same person taking eveything that is free... Also not much bumping here please... the things are free.. im guessing things wont be around long.. if there has been no activity for awhile, i will bump one last time and ask if its taken or no longer available.. if i get no answer the items & post will be deleted. I would like to keep this clean & easy. Some of the drama in these groups gets out of hand. Its not needed. We are adults! Ive seen this group work for other counties so I hope for the same. Let me know if you have questions

rules:
Your request to participate is pending approval.

name: The Talk of Port Clinton (uncensored)
rules:
Please feel free to share anything you feel about Port Clinton, Ohio and the surrounding area on here. This group respects all opinions.
Please do not troll the group by copying and pasting national news articles to spam the group and clutter it with stories that consume television. However you can express your thoughts and opinions in a post with a national news story if you communicate how it translates locally (Expressing your own thoughts, beliefs, and opinions)
Uncensored means the individual has the power to block those that they consider rude, condescending, nasty, no respect, and curse. The page does not dictate what can be said, it is up to the user to control what they like and what they do not. Freedom is great and you get to exercise it on this page. "YOU" have the power...you choose what offends you..not someone else.
If someone violates the rules of Facebook, please report them to Facebook. The administrator does not work for Facebook, which means he does not get paid to monitor and uphold the rules of Facebook. It's not his job, he just created a group on their website.
If you have a problem, please report it to the website not the administrator because only the employees of this website can enforce the rules of Faceboo

name: What's going on in and around Akron, Ohio
rules:
The place to post, read, and discuss fun new things happening in Akron, Ohio and the Greater Akron Area. Announce your festivals, new arts shows, and clubs and service organizations that serve our neighbors. Find a great spot to take the kids...share it here! See a sign for a local grand opening...let us know! Got a favorite local dining or shopping place that isn't a national chain...share it! And don't forget to add a picture to your posts. Former Akronites living elsewhere are also welcome to join us for newer views of Akron landmarks and businesses. Just be sure your public profile shows some prior Akron-centric info so we know you are really interested in us.
(the following added 12/29/15)
It may seem tempting to join this group for the purpose of posting free ads for your personally owned business. Don't...unless that business is clearly described as an entertainment/dining venue, non-profit, special event, or newly opened non-franchised locally based small business. Exceptions may be allowed if you include a special perk or discount for members of this group in exchange for posting no more than one mention of your business each month. If in doubt, ask first.
